content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule. Looking at atom O:31 (oxygen), I can see it's directly bonded to:

- N:30 (nitrogen) - the blue atom directly connected to O:31
- C:25 (carbon) - the yellow atom that appears to be directly connected to O:31 as well

From this image, I can clearly see that O:31 has two direct covalent bonds:
1. To N:30 (nitrogen)
2. To C:25 (carbon)

The bond between O:31 and C:25 is visible as a direct connection (yellow line), and O:31 and N:30 are also directly connected.

Regarding the geomconf_index, based on the file name "geomconf_8378.xyz", the geomconf_index appears to be 8378.

Let me confirm this by checking if there are any other neighbors I might have missed. The oxygen atom O:31 appears to have exactly two covalent bonds in this molecular structure.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom 31 (O) are:
- Atom index 30, element N (nitrogen)
- Atom index 25, element C (carbon)

And the geomconf_index is 8378.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 31, whose element should be O. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
